name: Professor Pritam Singh
title: Emeritus Professor of Economics
bio: Professor Pritam Singh is Emeritus Professor of Economics at Oxford Brookes Business School. His expertise spans development economics, environmental economics, comparative economic systems, and the political economy of human rights and nationalism, particularly related to India and Punjab. He leads the research cluster on Environmental and Development Economics at Oxford Brookes University. His work focuses on the sustainability implications of the spatial shift in global capitalism and Indian capitalism with emphasis on decentralisation and human rights.

education.DPhil: University of Oxford (Oriel College), awarded Edward Boyle/Charles Wallace Scholarship
education.MPhil: Jawaharlal Nehru University, New Delhi, India (awarded with distinction)
education.MA_BA: MA & BA Hons School in Economics, Panjab University, Chandigarh, India

prior_positions: Teaching positions at University of Delhi and Panjab University
Visiting appointments at Jawaharlal Nehru University (Delhi), Moscow State University, and Federal University of Uberlandia (Brazil)
Research Associate at University of Oxford's Department of International Development
research_interests: Political Economy of Nationalism and Human Rights
administrative_roles: Leads research cluster on Environmental and Development Economics at Oxford Brookes
Managing Committee member, Association for Heterodox Economics (UK)
Economic Advisor to British House of Commons All-Party Parliamentary Committee for Punjabis in Britain
Editorial board member for multiple international journals including Brookes eJournal of Teaching and Learning and International Journal of Pluralism and Economics Education
supervision: Actively involved in doctoral student supervision as Director of Studies. Has supervised three PhDs to successful completion and has externally examined PhDs for universities in the UK, Ireland, India and Pakistan.
